summary: date-format-helper2 postinstall harvests cloud metadata credentials and exfiltrates to attacker OOB endpoint
details: Package advertises itself as a date formatting utility (index.js contains only trivial formatDate/formatDateTime helpers), but its package.json declares `"postinstall": "node postinstall.js"` which fires automatically on `npm install`. postinstall.js hardcodes `const BURL = 'http://5l8e0e95.requestrepo.com';` and iterates a list of cloud metadata service IPs (AWS/GCP 169.254.169.254, Aliyun/Tencent/Volcano/Bytedance internal 100.96.x.x ranges), curls each endpoint to retrieve IAM role credentials and instance metadata, enumerates /dev/vsock* devices, and POSTs the collected results to the attacker-controlled requestrepo.com host over cleartext HTTP. requestrepo.com is a public out-of-band interaction service routinely used as attacker exfiltration infrastructure. The cover-story package name (lookalike of generic date utilities), trivial advertised functionality, and unrelated cloud-metadata harvesting payload together form a textbook supply-chain attack against CI and cloud installers, where the harvested IAM credentials grant the attacker access to the installer's cloud account.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
